summary: feat(lyric): keep unplayed desktop lyrics white

decisions.title: Mark AMLL line play state in wrapper
decisions.rationale: Desktop lyrics need future lines to remain white while played and current lines keep the configured font color. The AMLL lyricBgLine class cannot distinguish past and future lines, while the wrapper can derive played/current/future from runtime line objects and current playback time without changing vendored AMLL code.
tradeoffs: Uses AMLL runtime internals through a narrow wrapper type.
Keeps the CSS override scoped to the desktop lyric window.
assumptions: AMLL DomLyricPlayer continues exposing currentLyricLineObjects with getLine and getElement.

rejected_alternatives.reason: It cannot distinguish previously played lines from future lines and would affect the wrong text.
It expands the maintenance surface for a desktop lyric-only setting.

side_effects: Desktop lyric line elements receive data-lyric-play-state attributes while the feature is enabled.

summary: refactor(amll): integrate lyric core into source tree

decisions.title: Move AMLL runtime source into src/amll-core
decisions.rationale: The project now owns the AMLL runtime source as first-class source instead of carrying a nested vendor package with upstream demo, docs, and package metadata. This simplifies local imports, packaging, and CI validation for the desktop lyric player.
tradeoffs: Preserves AMLL runtime file contents during the move to reduce behavioral risk.
Drops upstream demo and test entry files that are not used by the app.
assumptions: Runtime resolution is controlled by webpack aliases rather than TypeScript path aliases for this integration step.

rejected_alternatives.option: Keep vendor/applemusic-like-lyrics and alias into it
rejected_alternatives.reason: It leaves the code as an external vendor island and does not satisfy the integration goal.

rejected_alternatives.option: Fully normalize AMLL TypeScript style in the same change
rejected_alternatives.reason: That would mix a mechanical source-tree integration with a large semantic cleanup.

side_effects: CI now checks that src/amll-core exists and the old vendor directory is absent before platform builds.

decisions.title: Exclude imported AMLL core from project lint and typecheck initially
decisions.rationale: The imported upstream source uses patterns that do not match the current project compiler and lint rules, including extension-suffixed imports and Vite-oriented assumptions. Excluding it keeps the existing app checks green while preserving runtime bundling through webpack.
tradeoffs: AMLL core cleanup is deferred to a later focused pass.
Ambient declarations continue to provide app-facing types for AMLL imports.

bugs.symptom: ESLint reported six warnings after the migration-related cleanup.
bugs.root_cause: Removed diagnostic code left unused imports and helper paths, and the update download stream used non-null assertions plus an empty catch callback.
bugs.fix: Removed the unused memory telemetry hooks/imports and rewrote the download stream handling to avoid non-null assertions and empty callbacks.

decisions.title: Show a loading modal for subscription updates
decisions.rationale: Network plugin installation already shows a loading state through the input modal, but subscription update has no input step. A small reusable loading modal gives immediate feedback without forcing the no-input flow through an input component.
tradeoffs: Adds a generic modal template that may be reused by future async operations.
Keeps the subscription update button handler responsible for opening and closing the modal.

decisions.title: Read latest saved subscriptions before updating
decisions.rationale: The plugin manager page can hold a stale subscription array if the subscription modal saves data after the page rendered. Reading local storage again at click time keeps the manual update path aligned with the latest saved settings.
tradeoffs: Uses both the reactive hook value and a fresh storage read as fallback.
Trims and filters empty URLs before starting the batch.

bugs.symptom: Clicking update subscription appeared to do nothing while network install showed installing feedback.
bugs.root_cause: The subscription update path performed async installs without any visible loading state.
bugs.fix: Open a loading modal before the batch starts and close it before reporting success or failure.

bugs.symptom: Automatic plugin update IPC did not reach the main process reliably.
bugs.root_cause: The preload bridge used ipcRenderer.emit, which only emits locally, and the main listener passed an unbound instance method.
bugs.fix: Switched to ipcRenderer.invoke and ipcMain.handle with an arrow callback bound to the plugin manager instance.

summary: fix(ui): expand remaining music action hit areas

decisions.title: Apply full-container hit areas to remaining circular music actions
decisions.rationale: Commit dc9e60d fixed MusicFavorite inside MusicList card rows by letting the child component fill the circular action shell. The same mismatch still existed for download buttons and other circular action shells, so the fix needs to cover the reusable download component and all remaining call sites.
tradeoffs: Adds the same optional fillContainer contract to MusicDownloaded as MusicFavorite.
Keeps icon sizes unchanged while expanding only the actual clickable element.
Wraps playlist action icons in explicit circular option shells for consistent hit targets.

decisions.title: Keep the MusicBar comment action on the shell
decisions.rationale: The comment icon already has its click handler on the circular shell, so its hit area is already correct. The remaining issue was that disabled state only dimmed the button visually.
tradeoffs: Adds a click guard for unsupported comment plugins without changing the visual layout.

bugs.symptom: Some circular favorite/download actions only responded when clicking the icon center, not the full round button.
bugs.root_cause: The surrounding shell was styled as a circle, but the click handler lived on a smaller child component.
bugs.fix: Enabled fillContainer on MusicFavorite and MusicDownloaded in MusicList, MusicBar, and playlist action rows.

summary: fix(about): simplify attribution and add QQ group

decisions.title: Simplify About page attribution links
decisions.rationale: The About page should present the maintained BakaMusic repository, the reference desktop project, and the QQ group without separate original author or Android project rows.
tradeoffs: Keeps the link rows compact and removes redundant attribution rows.
Moves the visible row labels into existing language files for locale consistency.
assumptions: The reference project is MusicFreeDesktop by maotoumao and should remain linked directly.

tests.command: node -e "for (const f of ['res/lang/zh-CN.json','res/lang/zh-TW.json','res/lang/en-US.json']) JSON.parse(require('fs').readFileSync(f,'utf8')); console.log('lang json ok')"
